page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 3800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 34 Gbps under the Nitro hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the m9gd.16xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple categories. Its multi-core CPU capabilities, measured via stress-ng and bzip3 decompression, rank in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory subsystem performance is also highly efficient, with both cached and uncached read/write memory bandwidth benchmarks placing in the top tier. Database and web operations are particularly strong, with Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput achieving top-tier results, alongside strong PassMark database operations.

From a resource density perspective, the inclusion of 3800 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage bundled directly with 64 dedicated ARM64 cores provides high qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive workloads. However, single-core CPU performance and cryptographic operations perform in the average tier, indicating that workloads relying heavily on single-threaded execution or intensive encryption may not experience the same relative performance gains. This hardware profile is highly suited for high-throughput databases, Redis caching layers, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 dedicated physical ARM64 cores powered by the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 256.0 GB of RAM, 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 34 Gbps. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, as evidenced by top 10% rankings in stress-ng multi-core, Redis SET, and static web serving benchmarks. While single-core and cryptographic performance sit in the average tier, the high-speed local storage and dedicated core density offer excellent qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive environments. The m9gd.16xlarge is highly suited for high-throughput databases, caching layers, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.16xlarge is a general-purpose ARM64 instance featuring 64 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V3 cores, 256 GB RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD local storage for high-throughput database and web workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.16xlarge features 64 dedicated ARM64 cores, 256 GB RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage for high-performance database workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 64-core ARM64 platform with high-speed local NVMe storage, optimized for high-throughput database and multi-threaded processing workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 at 2.8 GHz.
256 GB of system memory paired with 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and static web serving benchmark performance.
34 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Optimized for high-throughput databases, Redis caching, and multi-threaded data processing.
